2603.14007.title: Formal Abductive Explanations for Navigating Mental Health Help-Seeking and Diversity in Tech Workplaces
2603.14007.paper_summary: This work proposes a formal abductive explanation framework designed to systematically uncover rationales underlying AI predictions of mental health help-seeking within tech workplace settings. By computing rigorous justifications for model outputs, this approach enables principled selection of models tailored to distinct psychiatric profiles and underpins ethically robust recourse planning. Beyond moving past ad-hoc interpretability, we explicitly examine the influence of sensitive attributes such as gender on model decisions, a critical component for fairness assessments. In doing so, it aligns explanatory insights with the complex landscape of workplace mental health, ultimately supporting trustworthy deployment and targeted interventions.
2603.14007.paper_summary_zh: 這項工作提出了一個正式的推論解釋框架，旨在系統性地揭示在科技工作場所中，AI 對心理健康求助的預測背後的理由。<lb/>通過計算模型輸出的嚴謹理由，這種方法使得能夠根據不同的精神病學特徵，原則性地選擇量身定制的模型，並支撐倫理上堅實的應對計劃。<lb/>除了超越臨時的可解釋性，我們明確檢視了性別等敏感屬性對模型決策的影響，這是公平評估的關鍵組成部分。<lb/>在這樣做的過程中，它將解釋性見解與工作場所心理健康的複雜格局對齊，最終支持可信的部署和有針對性的干預措施。
2603.14007.author: Belona Sonna et.al.
2603.14007.authors: Belona Sonna, Alain Momo, Alban Grastien
